market_context: Risk-on tech tape ('Nasdaq Gains Over 200 Points Following Inflation Report', 07-15) fighting a memory-chip selloff ('SanDisk Sinks 13%', 07-15) and AI-fatigue chatter; VOO 693.50, near the top of its 30d range. Data pull PARTIAL for the 8th consecutive run: fundamentals failed for all 7 tickers (proxy 403), prices+news intact — sector caps remain unenforceable, so any buy would be rejected sector-unknown. Every ticker's headline list was non-empty today. Portfolio is 100% cash, so the holdings-only breaking-news web sweep was not applicable. Earnings dominate: JNJ beat-and-raised this morning yet closed down ~2.6% (sell-the-news); UNH prints Thursday 07-17; TSMC/ASML this week set the AI-demand tone. All holds on a one-source (Benzinga snapshot) day.

signals.thesis: Two hard positives — 'Apple Intelligence Finally Cleared for China' and a 52-week high (both 07-15) — offset by The Information's report that the Baltra AI server chip slipped past its 2026 ship target, forcing NVIDIA reliance. Price 327.39, top of the 30d range after a +4% day; chasing a high on a one-source day fails the entry bar. Falsified toward buy on a second independent China-rollout datapoint; toward sell if Baltra delay widens or China approval stalls in practice.

signals.thesis: Buffett claiming the Alphabet stake ('I Initiated It', 07-15) is a quality endorsement, but Play-store economics are live risk: 'Google to Allow Third-Party Stores in Its App Store Next Week' (Bloomberg, 07-15) plus FT's 'Brussels prepares fresh Google fines'. Price 371.06, near the 30d high after a 3-session +5% run. Falsified toward sell on quantified Play take-rate erosion or a formal EU fine; toward buy if third-party stores land without revenue impact.

signals.thesis: Q2 beat-and-raise this morning: adj EPS $2.90 vs $2.85 est, sales $25.31B beat, FY26 EPS guidance lifted to $11.60-$11.75 ('Finds Growth Beyond Legacy Drugs, Lifts 2026 Outlook', 07-15) — yet the stock fell ~2.6% to 247.11, third straight down day. A beat met by selling is not buy evidence on a one-source day, and sector-unknown data would get a buy rejected anyway. Falsified toward buy if price stabilizes above 250 with a second source confirming guidance quality; toward sell below 240.
